sourceMovie.title: Her
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Her (2013) as a romance theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The romance and science fiction blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Mr. Nobody
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Mr. Nobody draws from the same romance well as Her, channeling science-fiction in ways that feel unmistakably related. The 2009 film makes a natural companion watch — similar in spirit but distinctly its own.

recommendations.title: Starman
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Starman (1984) captures the romance appeal of Her without simply imitating it. The science-fiction streak running through both films is what puts Starman at the top of any "if you liked…" shortlist.

recommendations.title: The In Between
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The romance appeal of Her — that bittersweet edge, the science-fiction — translates cleanly into The In Between (2022). Different setting, same essential satisfaction.

recommendations.title: Ex Machina
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The filmmakers behind Ex Machina (2015) clearly understood what makes science-fiction work — the same quality that keeps Her rewatchable. Expect the same tense rhythm, executed with a fresh angle.

recommendations.title: A.I. Artificial Intelligence
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: A.I. Artificial Intelligence (2001) is the kind of film Her fans discover and immediately send to their group chat. The science-fiction overlap is real, but it's the tense emotional pull that seals the recommendation.

recommendations.title: Finch
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Finch shares Her's gift for science-fiction — the 2021 film delivers drama without overstaying its welcome. Consider it required viewing once you've finished rewatching your first pick.

recommendations.title: It Could Happen to You
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Much of what makes Her compelling — the romance, the drama — shows up in It Could Happen to You (1994) in a slightly different form. Both films understand that funny storytelling works best when it trusts the audience.

recommendations.title: Archive
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The science-fiction energy of Her is alive in Archive (2020), filtered through a different story but the same emotional register. It earns its place on this list with a tense payoff that rivals the original pick.

recommendations.title: Swan Song
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Swan Song (2021) operates in the same science-fiction as Her, but with its own distinct voice and pacing. The tense payoff will feel satisfyingly familiar without retreading the same ground.

recommendations.title: After Yang
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: After Yang (2022) belongs to the same conversation as Her — both anchor their science-fiction in drama emotion that doesn't let go. The tense undercurrent connects them more than any plot summary would suggest.

faq.question: What should I watch after Her?
faq.answer: Try Mr. Nobody, Starman, The In Between—each shares DNA with Her while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Her?
faq.answer: Her (2013) is best described as Romance, Science Fiction, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Her worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.8/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this romance lane.
